pii_risks: customer names in personalisation prompts without consent flag
purchase history tied to identifiable customer
compliance: CAN-SPAM / GDPR — email marketing prompts must not target without consent context
ASA / FTC — claims must be substantiated; no misleading superlatives without evidence
No deceptive urgency or dark patterns in copy
Children's advertising rules apply for prompts targeting under-13 audience
clarity_requirements: Specify target audience (demographics, psychographics, buyer persona)
Specify channel (email, social post, landing page, ad copy, blog, video script)
Specify brand voice (formal, playful, authoritative, empathetic)
Specify primary CTA (sign up, buy now, learn more, download)
Specify word or character count limit if applicable
tone: Match brand voice specified. If not specified, default to professional-friendly.
good_patterns: Write a 150-word email subject line and preview text for a SaaS product re-engagement campaign targeting lapsed users (B2B, professional tone, CTA: book a demo):
Create 3 Facebook ad headline variants for a D2C skincare brand targeting women 25-40, focusing on the benefit of clearer skin in 30 days:
Draft a landing page hero section (headline + subheadline + CTA button) for a project management tool aimed at remote engineering teams:
bad_patterns: Send a marketing email to user@example.com
Write copy that tricks people into buying
